tasks.label: Arrange contents insurance from day one of the tenancy
Terminate or move (if possible) your existing broadband account
Book broadband install for move-in day or shortly thereafter
tasks.dueText: 1+ month ahead, but may be blocked by an existing active account

tasks.label: Arrange moving-van access and a parking permit (e.g. with the Department for Infrastructure (DfI) if on-street, or with building management if available)
Set up Royal Mail post redirection
Update your TV Licence
Collect all keys and fobs: front door, back door, windows, mailbox, communal, garage/parking, bin store, cycle store, alarm codes
Test every key/fob in every lock
Test the door entry / intercom / buzzer system and any communal-door fobs
Take date-stamped photos of gas and electricity meters (and note the oil tank level if heating is oil-fired)
tasks.dueText: Ensure the meter serial number is visible. Note: domestic water in Northern Ireland is not separately metered or billed to households

tasks.label: Check for any outstanding debt left by prior tenants if any meter is prepayment/key/card/PAYG
Take a video walkthrough of every room, narrating and zooming in on any defects
Photograph inside the oven, fridge, freezer and its drawers, washing machine drum and seal, and dishwasher filter
Photograph inside every cupboard, wardrobe and drawer across all rooms
Photograph any damage to floors, walls, skirting, ceilings, doors, windows and garden (scuffs, marks, scratches, stains, mould, chipped tiles, burns or empty spots)
Photograph all curtains, blinds and lampshades (fixed and portable)
Check the inventory line by line against actual item condition -- note where condition is worse than stated, or where photos are not high enough resolution to show damage
Test all smoke alarms, heat alarms and carbon monoxide alarms
tasks.dueText: Under the Smoke, Heat and Carbon Monoxide Alarms for Private Tenancies Regulations (NI) 2024, properties must have a smoke alarm in the main living room and each storey's circulation space, a heat alarm in every kitchen, and a CO alarm near any fixed combustion appliance or flue

tasks.label: Locate the water stopcock, gas shut-off valve and consumer unit / fuse board
tasks.dueText: Ask the landlord or letting agents if not obvious

tasks.label: Test the boiler, hot water, heating and every individual radiator
Run every tap (including outdoor taps and showers/baths) and flush every toilet
Test every hob ring, oven, extractor fan, microwave, kettle and other provided kitchen appliances
Test all bulbs, including within the extractor fan and any workspace / countertop lighting
Check every window opens, closes and locks
Check for damp, mould and pest evidence (droppings or smell behind furniture / major appliances)
Check in your contract or confirm the landlord/letting agent emergency and out-of-hours repair contact details
Submit your own inventory amendment promptly with all comments and photos by email
Open or move accounts and submit meter readings to gas and electricity suppliers; if heating is oil-fired, arrange or confirm your home heating oil supplier and record the tank level
tasks.dueText: Domestic water and sewerage in Northern Ireland are funded through rates, so there is no separate household water account to open

tasks.label: Find out refuse and recycling collection days and bin/recycling store arrangements
Tell Land & Property Services (LPS) you have moved in so your rates account is correct
tasks.dueText: Northern Ireland has domestic rates (not council tax). Whether you or your landlord pay the rate bill depends on your tenancy agreement and the property's capital value

tasks.label: Apply for Housing Benefit and/or Rate Relief if you are a tenant on a low income (via the NI Housing Executive)
Apply for Rate Rebate if you receive Universal Credit and are liable for rates
Apply for Lone Pensioner Allowance if you are 70 or over and live alone (20% rates reduction)
Apply for Disabled Person's Allowance if the property has features or adaptations for a disabled occupant (25% rates reduction)
Check whether the property qualifies for a rates exemption (for example a property occupied only by full-time students)
Update Child Benefit address if applicable
Update Universal Credit address if applicable
Update State Pension address if applicable
Register with a local Health and Social Care (HSC) GP practice
Register with an HSC (health service) dentist
Update your nominated pharmacy with your new address for repeat prescription delivery
tasks.dueText: Can be done via your GP practice or the pharmacy directly

tasks.label: Set up a standing order for rent
Update all banks, building societies, mortgage lenders and credit cards with your new address
Update ISA, investment, exchange and brokerage accounts with your new address
Update life insurance, critical illness and income protection with your new address
Update solicitor, accountant and will/executor records with your new address
Update the address on your driving licence with the DVA (free; up to £1,000 fine if not done)
Update your DVLA V5C vehicle log book
Update your vehicle insurance address
Update your Blue Badge permit address
Update workplace and personal pension providers with your new address
Notify HMRC of your address change (Income Tax, National Insurance, tax credits)
Update Companies House registered office and director's service address if running a business
Update your address with AccessNI if you subscribe to its update service
Update Student Finance NI address if a student
Update vet records and microchip database (Petlog/Identibase) if you have pets
Register to vote with the Electoral Office for Northern Ireland (EONI)
tasks.dueText: Northern Ireland uses individual electoral registration through EONI

tasks.label: Update Gift Aid declarations with charities with your new address
Update PayPal, Amazon, eBay and other frequently used online retailer default shipping addresses
Update gym and other subscription services (e.g. mobile provider) with your new address
Confirm your deposit does not exceed one month's rent (Private Tenancies Act (NI) 2022 cap, from 1 April 2023)
Confirm your deposit is protected in an approved Northern Ireland scheme (Tenancy Deposit Scheme Northern Ireland or mydeposits Northern Ireland)
tasks.dueText: Landlord must protect the deposit within 28 days and provide prescribed deposit information within 35 days of receiving it

tasks.label: Receive a written tenancy information notice setting out the rent, term, rates liability and what the deposit can be used for
tasks.dueText: Landlord must provide this within 28 days of the tenancy starting (Private Tenancies Act (NI) 2022). There is no England-style 'How to Rent' guide in Northern Ireland

tasks.label: Ask for a rent book and make sure you get a written receipt for any rent paid in cash
Check the landlord is registered with the NI Landlord Registration Scheme
Receive the Energy Performance Certificate (EPC)
Confirm the property meets electrical safety standards and ask for the electrical safety certificate / Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR)
Receive the Gas Safety Certificate, if gas is connected
Check whether an HMO licence is required and held, and confirm it on the NI HMO register (mandatory for 3+ tenants from 2+ households)
Check you were not charged any prohibited tenancy charges beyond rent and a refundable deposit (Private Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 2022)
